judgment.summary: Strong growth and returns are offset by a high valuation and thin margins.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimate by 0.8%; next earnings date is 2026-05-28.
positives: Revenue growth 21.5% YoY and earnings growth 45.5% YoY show strong top and bottom-line expansion.
Return on equity at 29.7% and return on assets at 8.7% indicate efficient use of capital.
Free cash flow of $6.69B provides ample liquidity.
Analyst consensus is a buy with a mean target suggesting slight upside potential.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 56.83 and forward P/E of 47.64 are very high, indicating a premium valuation.
PEG ratio of 5.56 suggests the stock may be overvalued relative to its expected earnings growth.
Operating margin of 3.7% and profit margin of 3.0% are relatively thin for a retail business.
Debt/Equity ratio of 60.26 indicates a moderate level of leverage.

news.paragraph: Costco is highlighted as a top long-term, low-risk stock pick by hedge funds, suggesting positive institutional sentiment. While no specific catalysts are mentioned in this news, the upcoming earnings report on May 28th will be a key event to watch for any changes in outlook. Traders should consider the current high valuation relative to analyst growth estimates.

headlines.title: Costco (COST) – Among the 10 Best Long Term Low Risk Stocks to Buy According to Hedge Funds
